refactor: modernize loops in merge() and object.vals() for better performance
prs.body:
**Summary**
This PR modernizes legacy ES5-style loops in lib/utils.js (specifically merge() and object.vals()) using modern for...of syntax.

**Motivation**
Mongoose contains several internal utility functions using manual index management (while loops with i++). Modern V8 engines (Node.js 16+) have optimized for...of to the point where they are now often faster and significantly more readable than manual indexing.

**Performance Benchmarks**
Tested on Node.js 20.x:
| Case | Old (ops/sec) | New (ops/sec) | Improvement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| merge() - Small | 1,527,137 | 1,636,612 | +6.7% |
| merge() - Large | 7,776 | 7,932 | +2.0% |
| object.vals() | 1,566,261 | 1,604,254 | +2.4% |

**Examples**
No API changes. This is a pure internal performance and maintainability refactor. All 26 utility tests and the broader Mongoose test suite pass.

prs.title: refactor: rename window.mode.isMobile() to window.mode.isSmallScreenDevice()
prs.body:
* **Resolves:** #14140
* **Target version:** main

### Summary
This PR renames the internal identifier `window.mode.isMobile()` to `window.mode.isSmallScreenDevice()` across the JavaScript and TypeScript layers. 

**Motivation:**
The term "mobile" is increasingly ambiguous. Renaming this to `isSmallScreenDevice` clarifies that the logic is checking for **available screen real estate** (responsive breakpoints) rather than the specific **hardware type**. This improves developer experience and semantic clarity.

**Technical Details:**
- Updated 54 files, including core definitions in `global.js` and `global.d.ts`.
- **Note on CSS:** I have intentionally kept the `.mobile` CSS class toggles (`addClass('mobile')`) intact to avoid high-risk visual regressions in the stylesheet library, while still achieving the goal of clarifying the underlying JS/TS logic.

prs.title: fix(Treemap): support functional dataKey using getValueByDataKey
prs.body:
## Problem

In `Treemap.tsx`, `dataKey` extraction currently uses direct property lookup:
`const rawNodeValue = node[dataKey as string];`

This causes the Treemap to fail (returning `NaN` or incorrect values) if a `dataKey` is passed as a function—a feature that is standard and supported across most other Recharts components.

## Fix

Replaced the direct property lookup with the standard `getValueByDataKey` utility in `computeNode`. This standardizes `Treemap` with the rest of the library and enables support for:

  - Formatter/Accessor functions
  - Nested object paths (e.g., `data.value`)
  - Array indices

## Verification (Proof of Work)

I have verified this fix with a new regression test in `test/chart/Treemap.spec.tsx`.

**Test Case Details:**

  - **Input Data:** `[{ name: 'A', baseValue: 10 }, { name: 'B', baseValue: 20 }]`
  - **Functional dataKey:** `dataKey={(node) => (node.baseValue || 0) * 2}`
  - **Verified Result:** Node 'A' correctly rendered with value `20` and Node 'B' with `40`.

**Checks Performed:**

  - [x] `npm run test -- test/chart/Treemap.spec.tsx` passed (22/22 tests).
  - [x] `check-types-lib` passed.
  - [x] Resolves the internal `// TODO need to verify dataKey` comment in `Treemap.tsx`.

-----

Fix: Complete missing translations for German locale
prs.body:
# 📝 Fix: Missing German Translations for New Features

## Problem
Several newer features were missing translation entries in the German locale file (`de.js`). As a result, users switching to German saw untranslated English strings across versioning, view modes, caching, schema management, and file operations.

This caused incomplete localization and an inconsistent UI experience.

---

## Solution
Added all missing German translations and aligned placeholders with the English locale. All new entries were translated accurately, checked for grammatical correctness, and made consistent with existing German terminology in the project.

---

## ✅ Changes Included

### 🔧 Schema & Validation

### 🕒 Versioning System

- Confirmed UI updates correctly when switching to the German language
- Ensured no key collisions or missing objects

---

This update restores full translation coverage for German users and improves UI consistency across the application.

prs.title: Add system theme preference support using respectPrefersColorScheme
prs.body:
This PR enables **automatic theme detection** based on the user's operating system settings.

By adding:
```js
respectPrefersColorScheme: true
```

he site now respects the user's system-level dark or light mode on their first visit, aligning with the “System Default” theme behavior.

**✅ What’s Already Working**

Theme toggle button (enabled by default)
Light/Dark mode switching
localStorage persistence
Dark mode logos configured

**🔧 What’s New**
Added respectPrefersColorScheme: true to automatically set theme based on OS preference

**🧪 Testing**

Open the site in an incognito/private window.
Change your system theme to dark/light mode.
Reload the page — it should match your OS theme automatically.

prs.title: Add Light/Dark theme selector to web UI with Monaco sync and persistence
prs.body: This PR adds a user-selectable Light/Dark theme to the KidCode web interface and keeps the editor theme in sync. The selection is persisted across sessions. - Added a theme dropdown (Light / Dark) in the top-right controls of the editor panel. - Implemented dark-mode styles for the entire web UI (panels, headers, modal, output log, canvas border/background). - Synchronized Monaco Editor theme to vs-light/vs-dark based on selection. - Persisted theme choice in localStorage (kidcode.theme) and applied on initial load. - Kept full keyboard accessibility; selector is labelled and reachable via tab. **Changed files:** - kidcode-web/src/main/resources/static/index.html - kidcode-web/src/main/resources/static/style.css - kidcode-web/src/main/resources/static/app.js It Fixes the issue no. #69 ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Theme selector: new header control to toggle between light and dark themes (accessible label and visual switch). * Dark theme: comprehensive dark-mode styling across UI, panels, dialogs, editor, canvases, and outputs. * Theme persistence & sync: chosen theme is saved, restored and applied immediately on load to minimize visual flash; editor theme follows the selection.

issues.title: [Feature Request] Add Copy Button to Code Snippets in Documentation
issues.body:
### Description

In the [Three.js manual](https://threejs.org/manual/#en/installation) and other documentation pages, there are multiple code snippet boxes.  
Currently, users need to manually select and copy the code, which can be slightly inconvenient — especially for beginners who want to quickly test examples.


### Solution

Add a small "Copy" button on the top-left (or top-right) corner of each code snippet box.
Clicking the button would instantly copy the snippet to the clipboard and show a short confirmation message (like “Copied!”).

This would make it easier for users to quickly copy and try code samples directly in their projects.

### Alternatives

Currently, users can manually select and copy the text. However, this is slower and prone to errors, especially for longer snippets.
Adding a dedicated copy button is a lightweight and user-friendly enhancement that aligns with modern documentation UX practices.

### Additional context

Many developer documentation sites (like MDN, React Docs, and Docusaurus) already have a similar feature to improve the developer experience.
This addition would make the Three.js documentation more accessible, beginner-friendly, and aligned with current web standards.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Cmd+K / Ctrl+K Shortcut to Open Search
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**  
Currently, the MathJax documentation already includes a search button in the top navigation.  
However, there’s no keyboard shortcut to open the search box quickly.  
Most modern documentation sites allow pressing **Cmd + K (macOS)** or **Ctrl + K (Windows/Linux)** to instantly open the search dialog — this greatly improves accessibility and speed.  
It’s slightly inconvenient to manually click the search icon each time.

**Describe the solution you'd like**  
Add a global keyboard shortcut:  
- **Cmd + K (macOS)**  
- **Ctrl + K (Windows/Linux)**  

This shortcut should trigger the existing search modal or input box without requiring a mouse click.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**  
- Manually clicking the search icon (works, but slower).  
- Using browser search (Ctrl + F), which only searches the current page.

**Additional context**  
Implementing this would align MathJax Docs with the UX standards of modern documentation frameworks such as Docusaurus, VitePress, and Next.js Docs, which all support **Cmd + K** to open search.

### Title

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Currently, the MathJax documentation site (https://docs.mathjax.org/en/latest/index.html) does not support switching between light and dark themes. This can make reading or referencing documentation uncomfortable in different lighting conditions, especially for users who prefer dark mode for accessibility or eye comfort.

---

Add a theme toggler button to the documentation UI, allowing users to switch between **light mode** and **dark mode**.  
Optionally, it could:
- Respect the system’s color scheme preference (`prefers-color-scheme: dark`).
- Remember the user’s theme choice using local storage or cookies.

This would significantly improve the readability and user experience of the documentation.

---

### Describe alternatives you've considered
- Using browser extensions to force dark mode (inconsistent results and often break page styling).
- Using reader mode (loses important formatting and navigation).

---

### Additional context
Many modern documentation sites (like Docusaurus, VitePress, and Sphinx-based docs) now include theme togglers by default. Adding this feature to MathJax docs would bring it in line with current best practices for developer documentation.

The documentation site would benefit from a **theme toggler (Light/Dark mode)** to enhance user experience and give the interface a more polished and modern appearance.

- Reduces eye strain for users browsing at night

- Detect system preference with `prefers-color-scheme`.  
- Apply a `.dark` class to the root or body element to toggle themes.  
- Maintain accessibility standards (contrast ratio, focus outlines, etc.).

A modern, user-friendly documentation interface with seamless theme switching that improves accessibility, comfort, and overall design consistency.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Theme Toggler to Documentation Site

# Feature Request: Add "Copy to Clipboard" Button for Code Blocks in Theatre.js Documentation

When following the [Theatre.js documentation](https://www.theatrejs.com/docs/), there are several code examples and setup commands  for example, in the installation and starter code sections  but **no “Copy” button** is provided for the code blocks.  

This makes it slightly inconvenient for users who want to quickly copy shell commands or example code when setting up their projects.

---

## **Example Sections Without a Copy Button**

### Installing dependencies

The docs currently show installation commands like this:

```bash
# r3f and its dependencies
npm install --save react three @react-three/fiber

# Theatre.js
npm install --save @theatre/core@0.5 @theatre/studio@0.5 @theatre/r3f@0.5

# Three.js types (when using TypeScript)

```

## Having a small clipboard icon button next to each of these code blocks would allow users to quickly copy the full command without manually selecting text.

---

## R3F Starter Code Example

Similarly, there’s no “Copy” button for the code snippets like the following:

> To start, we'll create a `main.tsx` file containing the code in the block below.  
> For example, if you're using the Vite React-TS starter, you can replace the entire contents of the `main.tsx` file with this:

```tsx
import * as THREE from 'three'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import React, { useRef, useState } from 'react'
import { Canvas, useFrame } from '@react-three/fiber'
import { getProject } from '@theatre/core'

// our Theatre.js project sheet, we'll use this later
const demoSheet = getProject('Demo Project').sheet('Demo Sheet')

const App = () => {
  return (
    <Canvas
      camera={{
        position: [5, 5, -5],
        fov: 75,
      }}
    >
      <ambientLight />
      <pointLight position={[10, 10, 10]} />
      <mesh>
        <boxGeometry args={[1, 1, 1]} />
        <meshStandardMaterial color="orange" />
      </mesh>
    </Canvas>
  )
}

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(<App />)
```
Adding a **copy-to-clipboard button** here would be extremely helpful for users following along with the guide.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Add a small **"Copy" clipboard icon button** to the top-right corner of each code block in the docs.

### Expected behavior

- When the user clicks the button, the code block text is copied to their clipboard.  
- A tooltip (e.g.,  “Copied!”) appears briefly to confirm the action.  
- Uses the browser’s built-in Clipboard API (`navigator.clipboard.writeText`).

### Visual inspiration
Similar behavior can be seen in the documentation sites for:
- [React](https://react.dev/)
- [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/)
- [TailwindCSS](https://tailwindcss.com/)

---

## Describe alternatives you've considered

- Manually selecting and copying text (default behavior).  
  However, this is slower, can cause errors (like copying extra characters), and reduces the smoothness of onboarding for users.

---

## Additional context

This enhancement will make the [Theatre.js documentation](https://www.theatrejs.com/docs/) more polished, modern, and beginner-friendly.

I’d love to **work on this feature** and contribute a PR to add copy buttons across all code blocks!

issues.title: Add theme toggle (light/dark mode switch) to the Theatre.js website
issues.body:
## Feature Request

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**  
The current [Theatre.js website](https://www.theatrejs.com) only has a single default theme (dark).  
While it looks great, it lacks a **light mode** option, which can affect readability or user comfort depending on ambient lighting and personal preference.

---

**Describe the solution you'd like**  
Add a **theme toggle button** (🌞 / 🌙) that allows switching between **light** and **dark** modes.

- Place the toggle in the top-right corner of the navigation bar.  
- Save user preference using `localStorage` so the theme persists on reload.  
- Default can remain dark mode for brand consistency.  
- Light mode would improve accessibility and offer a modern, polished feel.

---

**Describe alternatives you've considered**  
- Using the system preference via `prefers-color-scheme`.  
  However, providing a visible toggle gives users direct control and better UX.

---

**Teachability, Documentation, Adoption, Migration Strategy**  
This is a **non-breaking enhancement** for the documentation/landing site.  
It can be implemented by toggling a CSS class or using CSS variables.  
If the site uses Docusaurus, Next.js, or a similar framework, an existing light/dark theme switcher component can be integrated easily.

---

**Additional context**  
Many major documentation sites (e.g., React, TailwindCSS, Vite) already offer light/dark theme toggles.  
This addition would make the Theatre.js site more user-friendly and visually versatile.  

I’d love to **work on this enhancement** and open a PR if approved!

issues.title: Scroll to the top button on the right corner

type.name: Enhancement
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
### Use case

When navigating long pages such as exercise lists, nutrition diaries, or workout history, users often have to manually scroll back up to reach the top navigation or perform new actions. This becomes inconvenient on smaller screens or lengthy lists.

### Proposal

Add a “Scroll to Top” button (⬆️) fixed to the bottom-right corner of the screen.
When clicked, it smoothly scrolls the page to the top.

**Suggested behavior:**

- Only visible after scrolling down a certain distance.
- Uses a fade-in / fade-out transition for smooth UX.
- Can be implemented using a simple JavaScript/React hook or CSS scroll-behavior: smooth.

**Why this feature is needed**

- Improves usability on mobile and desktop devices.
- Reduces repetitive manual scrolling.
- Aligns with modern UI/UX design seen in many dashboard-style web apps.

### Additional Context

- Example: GitHub and Medium both implement similar scroll-to-top buttons.
- Could use an upward arrow icon (↑) or floating action button (FAB) style.
- Optional enhancement: animate only after the user scrolls past 400–600px.

issues.title: [Feature]: Add a Change Theme Button (Dark/Light Mode Toggle)
issues.body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** Currently, the interface is locked in dark mode. Users who prefer a light interface, or want to switch themes based on system preference, do not have the option to toggle between dark and light themes. **Describe the solution you'd like** Add a Change Theme button that allows users to toggle between dark and light modes. - The toggle can be placed in the top-right corner (e.g., next to the profile icon). - The preference should persist across sessions (stored in localStorage or user settings). - Smooth transition animations between themes would enhance UX. **Describe alternatives you've considered** - Automatically adapting the theme based on the user’s system preference (prefers-color-scheme). - Providing the theme toggle in the settings page instead of the header. **Additional context** Attached screenshot shows the current interface in dark mode where the toggle could be beneficial. ✅ **Prerequisite Checklist** - [x] ⭐ Starred [Helixque Repo](https://github.com/HXQLabs/Helixque/) - [x] ⭐ Starred [Helixque Landing Repo](https://github.com/HXQLabs/helixque-landing/) - [x] 📘 Read and understood [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/HXQLabs/Helixque/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] 💬 Joined [Discord Channel](https://discord.com/invite/dQUh6SY9Uk) - [x] 🌐 Explored and signed up on [helixque.netlify.app](https://helixque.netlify.app/)

issues.title: “Details” Box Should Expand to Full Available Width

issues.title: Add Sign In / Sign Up Page for Future Database Integration
issues.body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, the project has no user authentication system. There’s no Sign In or Sign Up page, which limits functionality for future personalized or data-driven features.

## Proposed Solution
Create a simple, responsive Sign In and Sign Up page UI.  
- These pages can be static for now (frontend only).  
- Later, once the backend and database are set up, they can be connected to handle actual authentication.  
- Include fields like:
  - **Email / Username**
  - **Password**
  - Optional: Confirm Password (for Sign Up)
- Include basic validation and feedback messages (e.g., invalid email format, password too short, etc.).

## Alternatives Considered
- Using a modal-based login instead of a dedicated page.  
- Deferring this feature until the backend is ready, but creating it early helps design and routing consistency.

## Additional Context
This feature will make it easier to integrate authentication later without redesigning the UI.  
It also helps in testing route protection and navigation flow early in development.
